questions.questionText: The city library added an online renewal system. Therefore, the line at the front desk will become shorter. Which assumption is necessary for the argument?

options.text: The library has more books than most nearby libraries.
A meaningful number of front-desk visits are made by patrons renewing books.
Online renewal systems are popular in large universities.
The library will reduce its staff after the system begins.

questions.shortExplanation: The conclusion depends on renewals being a real cause of front-desk lines.

steps.title: Find the conclusion
steps.text: The front-desk line will become shorter.

steps.title: Find the evidence
steps.text: The library added online renewals.

steps.title: Find the missing link
steps.text: Online renewals shorten lines only if renewals are one reason patrons stand in line.

fullExplanation.correctAnswer: B is required. If front-desk visits are not for renewals, online renewal would not necessarily shorten the line.

whyOtherOptionsAreWrong.A: The size of the collection does not connect online renewals to front-desk lines.
whyOtherOptionsAreWrong.C: Popularity elsewhere may support the idea, but it is not required.
whyOtherOptionsAreWrong.D: Staff reduction is not needed and could even make lines longer.

questions.commonMistake: Choosing a generally positive detail instead of the required bridge.

questions.questionText: A school cafeteria added a vegetarian lunch option. The principal concludes that fewer students will bring lunch from home. Which assumption does the conclusion depend on?

options.text: Some students bring lunch from home because the cafeteria previously lacked a vegetarian option.
Vegetarian meals cost more to prepare than other meals.
Students who bring lunch from home always eat with friends.
The cafeteria serves lunch at the same time every day.

questions.shortExplanation: The new option matters only if the old missing option caused some students to bring lunch.

steps.title: Find the conclusion
steps.text: Fewer students will bring lunch from home.

steps.title: Find the evidence
steps.text: The cafeteria added a vegetarian option.

steps.title: Find the missing link
steps.text: The option must address a reason students brought lunch from home.

fullExplanation.correctAnswer: A gives the required bridge between the new menu choice and the predicted behavior.

whyOtherOptionsAreWrong.B: Preparation cost does not explain student behavior.
whyOtherOptionsAreWrong.C: Eating with friends is unrelated to why students bring lunch.
whyOtherOptionsAreWrong.D: A fixed lunch time does not connect vegetarian options to home lunches.

questions.commonMistake: Focusing on cafeteria operations instead of the reason students behave a certain way.

questions.questionText: A company replaced its three-hour training session with six ten-minute videos. Managers claim employees will complete training faster. Which option is a necessary assumption, not just a tempting extra fact?

options.text: Employees prefer video lessons to live sessions.
The new videos cover at least the required training content.
Employees will spend less total required time on the new video format than on the old session.
The company has used online videos in other departments.

questions.shortExplanation: The conclusion is about faster completion, so total required time must actually be lower.

steps.title: Find the conclusion
steps.text: Employees will complete training faster.

steps.title: Find the evidence
steps.text: The format changed from a three-hour session to short videos.

steps.title: Find the missing link
steps.text: Shorter pieces do not guarantee less total training time unless the total required time is lower.

fullExplanation.correctAnswer: C is required because the argument depends on total training time decreasing.

whyOtherOptionsAreWrong.A: Preference may help completion, but it is not required for a time-based conclusion.
whyOtherOptionsAreWrong.B: Coverage matters for quality, but the conclusion is about speed.
whyOtherOptionsAreWrong.D: Prior use elsewhere is background, not a required assumption.

questions.commonMistake: Choosing a likable benefit, such as preference, instead of the time bridge.

questions.questionText: A study app now sends reminders thirty minutes before scheduled practice. The app team predicts that users will complete more practice sessions. Which assumption best fills the missing link?

options.text: Some users miss practice sessions because they forget them.
The app has a more colorful home screen than before.
Practice sessions are harder than review sessions.
Users who practice daily usually improve their scores.

questions.shortExplanation: Reminders help completion only if forgetfulness is one reason sessions are missed.

steps.title: Find the conclusion
steps.text: Users will complete more practice sessions.

steps.title: Find the evidence
steps.text: The app sends reminders before scheduled practice.

steps.title: Find the missing link
steps.text: Reminders solve missed sessions only when forgetting is part of the cause.

whyOtherOptionsAreWrong.B: Design color does not explain why reminders will increase completion.
whyOtherOptionsAreWrong.C: Difficulty is unrelated to the reminder mechanism.
whyOtherOptionsAreWrong.D: Score improvement may be true, but it does not show reminders increase completion.

questions.commonMistake: Selecting a positive learning outcome instead of the cause addressed by the evidence.

questions.questionText: The local clinic added evening appointment slots. The director concludes that missed appointments will decrease. Which choice is an assumption rather than an inference?

options.text: Some missed appointments occur because patients cannot attend during daytime hours.
The clinic is open later than it was before.
Some patients prefer evening appointments.
The clinic director wants more patients to attend appointments.

questions.shortExplanation: The conclusion needs a link between daytime access and missed appointments.

steps.title: Find the conclusion
steps.text: Missed appointments will decrease.

steps.title: Find the evidence
steps.text: Evening appointment slots were added.

steps.title: Find the missing link
steps.text: Evening slots help only if daytime-only scheduling caused some missed appointments.

fullExplanation.correctAnswer: A is required because it connects new appointment times to the reason appointments were missed.

whyOtherOptionsAreWrong.B: This follows from the evidence and is not the hidden support.
whyOtherOptionsAreWrong.C: Preference may help, but the conclusion is about missed appointments.
whyOtherOptionsAreWrong.D: The director's goal does not prove the prediction.

questions.commonMistake: Choosing an inference that follows from the facts instead of the assumption that supports the prediction.

questions.questionText: A tutoring center plans to send weekly progress messages to parents. The director says this will improve student attendance. Which assumption is necessary?

options.text: Parents read at least some of the progress messages they receive.
Tutoring centers usually send messages on Fridays.
Students enjoy receiving praise from teachers.
Attendance is the only factor that affects progress.

questions.shortExplanation: Messages cannot influence attendance if parents do not read them at all.

steps.title: Find the conclusion
steps.text: Student attendance will improve.

steps.title: Find the evidence
steps.text: Parents will receive weekly progress messages.

steps.title: Find the missing link
steps.text: The messages must reach and influence parents in some way.

fullExplanation.correctAnswer: A is necessary. If parents never read the messages, the proposed mechanism breaks.

whyOtherOptionsAreWrong.B: The day messages are sent is not required.
whyOtherOptionsAreWrong.C: The argument is about parents, not student praise.
whyOtherOptionsAreWrong.D: This is much too strong and not required.

questions.commonMistake: Choosing an extreme statement that overstates what the argument needs.

questions.questionText: The transport department will replace several diesel buses with electric buses. Officials conclude that the department's fuel spending will decline next year. Which assumption is necessary?

options.text: The electric buses will be used on routes that would otherwise require diesel fuel.
Electric buses are quieter than diesel buses.
Passengers prefer newer buses to older buses.
The department will hire more drivers next year.

questions.shortExplanation: Fuel spending falls only if electric buses replace actual diesel-fuel use.

steps.title: Find the conclusion
steps.text: Fuel spending will decline next year.

steps.title: Find the evidence
steps.text: Some diesel buses will be replaced with electric buses.

steps.title: Find the missing link
steps.text: The replacement must reduce diesel use, not simply add buses or shift unused vehicles.

fullExplanation.correctAnswer: A is required because it ties electric buses to displaced diesel fuel spending.

whyOtherOptionsAreWrong.B: Noise is irrelevant to fuel spending.
whyOtherOptionsAreWrong.C: Passenger preference does not determine fuel costs.
whyOtherOptionsAreWrong.D: More drivers may affect labor cost, not the stated fuel claim.

questions.commonMistake: Treating any benefit of electric buses as relevant to the exact cost claim.

questions.questionText: A project team adopted a shared task board. The manager concludes that fewer deadlines will be missed. Which assumption best supports the missing link?

options.text: At least some missed deadlines happen because team members are unclear about task ownership or due dates.
The shared task board has a modern visual design.
The team works on more than one project at a time.
Managers in other departments also use shared task boards.

questions.shortExplanation: The board helps deadlines only if unclear ownership or due dates caused missed deadlines.

steps.title: Find the conclusion
steps.text: Fewer deadlines will be missed.

steps.title: Find the evidence
steps.text: The team adopted a shared task board.

steps.title: Find the missing link
steps.text: The board must address a cause of missed deadlines.

fullExplanation.correctAnswer: A identifies the cause that the task board can fix.

whyOtherOptionsAreWrong.B: A modern design does not prove deadline improvement.
whyOtherOptionsAreWrong.C: Multiple projects may make the board useful, but it is not the required link.
whyOtherOptionsAreWrong.D: Use in other departments is not required for this team.

questions.commonMistake: Confusing a tool feature with the specific problem the tool must solve.
